title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police release surveillance photos seeking public help in identifying a suspect in a lemonade stand robbery.
The armed robbery on June 10 involved two juveniles; a 14-year-old was previously arrested and arraigned.
Authorities request public assistance via Boston Police District C-6 Detectives or CrimeStoppers for robbery information.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is exploring options for his next NBA team after leaving the Lakers, according to Rich Paul.
The Boston Celtics, noted on Rich Paul's board, are considered a less likely landing spot for LeBron James.
If James joins Boston, he'd fit a point-forward role alongside Derrick White, Paul George, Mitchell Robinson, and Jayson Tatum.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers, calling the move disturbing.
Parish compared the Brown trade to the Dallas Mavericks' controversial trade of Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
Parish expressed hope that Jaylen Brown and the Celtics could remain amicable and that Brown's jersey might be retired in the future.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum penned a farewell message to Jaylen Brown after Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown was traded for Paul George and multiple draft picks, despite not requesting a trade from the Celtics.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics, while Tatum wished him well in his career's next chapter.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extend Neemias Queta's contract four years for $56 million, through the 2030-31 season.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in the 2025-26 season.
Celtics picked up Queta's $2.67 million team option for the 2026-27 season before adding four-year extension.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston, established in 1826, is America's oldest continuously operating restaurant, celebrating its 200-year anniversary.
Owner Joseph Milano, since 1970, expanded the restaurant's capacity to serve up to 1,500 guests daily.
Union Oyster House's historical significance includes its 1716-1717 building and hosting figures like Daniel Webster and John F. Kennedy.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's busy summer includes the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4, featuring performances by Chance the Rapper and others.
A World Cup quarterfinal will be held at Boston Stadium on July 9, marking the last match hosted in the city this summer.
Sail Boston’s Parade of Sail begins July 11, with over 60 tall ships sailing into Boston Harbor to mark America's 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, the oldest continuously operating restaurant in the U.S., has hosted six presidents, including JFK and Obama.
Celebrities like Matt Damon, Al Pacino, and Meryl Streep are among the famous who have dined at Boston's Union Oyster House.
Owner Joseph Milano commissioned a 2003 painting depicting over 50 of the restaurant's legendary guests socializing together.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, died unexpectedly; he was 41.
Ross held roles in the Major Crime Unit and was commander of the Collision Analysis Unit.
Funeral services are scheduled for July 7 at the United Church of Christ in North Hampton.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized Boston Celtics for lack of respect after being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Celtics received Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks in exchange for Brown.
Brown expressed disappointment over unclear communication regarding the trade from Celtics' management.
